events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Legendary Beatles drummer Ringo Starr was born on July 7, 1940, in Liverpool.

angles.name: Ringo's 86th
angles.copy: The legendary Ringo Starr turns 86 today! From The Beatles to his All-Starr Band, what's your favourite Ringo moment?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's iconic guitarist and astrophysicist Brian May was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Brian May's 79th
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! Guitar legend, astrophysicist, animal welfare advocate – is there anything he can't do?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones frontman Mick Jagger was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Mick Jagger's 83rd
angles.copy: Still strutting after all these years! Mick Jagger celebrates his 83rd birthday today. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first broadcast music video.

angles.name: MTV's 45th Anniversary
angles.copy: It's 45 years since MTV changed the game, launching with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. Did video really kill the radio star? We think not!

events.title: Oasis release Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which celebrates its 32nd anniversary.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe 32nd
angles.copy: Thirty-two years ago, Oasis dropped their debut masterpiece 'Definitely Maybe'. What's your favourite track from the album that launched a thousand singalongs?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in counterculture history, took place from August 15-18, 1969.

angles.name: Woodstock Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago today, the legendary Woodstock Festival kicked off. Three days of peace, love, and music that defined a generation. Which artist would you have loved to see there?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977, and would have been 91.

angles.name: Remembering Elvis
angles.copy: Remembering the King, Elvis Presley, 49 years since his passing. He would have been 91. His music continues to inspire generations.

events.title: Remembering Tupac Shakur
events.blurb: Remembering influential rapper Tupac Shakur, who was tragically shot in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996, and would have been 55.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: We remember the enduring legacy of Tupac Shakur, who would have been 55 today. A true icon whose words still resonate decades on.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: Remembering the legendary guitarist Jimi Hendrix, who passed away on September 18, 1970, and would have been 84.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Tribute
angles.copy: Today, we remember the unparalleled talent of Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84. His innovative guitar work and influence on rock music remain monumental.

events.title: The Beatles release Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic eleventh studio album 'Abbey Road' on September 26, 1969, featuring tracks like 'Come Together' and 'Something'.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us the legendary 'Abbey Road'. That zebra crossing is still one of the most famous album covers ever! What's your favourite track?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' frontwoman Chrissie Hynde was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde's 75th
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon whose voice and attitude have inspired for decades.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis frontman Liam Gallagher was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Liam Gallagher's 54th
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The voice of a generation, still bringing the rock and roll swagger. As you were!

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon released his iconic solo album 'Imagine' on September 9, 1971, featuring the timeless title track.

angles.name: Imagine 55th Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-five years ago, John Lennon gave us 'Imagine'. A powerful message of peace and unity that still resonates globally. What does the song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the original iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music players.

angles.name: iPod 25th Anniversary
angles.copy: Remember when the iPod changed how we listened to music forever? It's 25 years since Apple dropped the original. How many songs did you cram onto yours?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day celebrates the premiere of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', on October 5, 1962.

angles.name: Happy James Bond Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 007 and over six decades of iconic espionage, thrilling action, and unforgettable theme tunes. Shaken, not stirred, of course.

events.title: John Lennon turns 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles co-founder John Lennon was born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool.

angles.name: John Lennon's 86th
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon on what would have been his 86th birthday. His music and message continue to inspire peace and love around the world.
